free_text: Thick target yields of 34m-Cl and 38-Cl produced by
various charged particles on phosphorus, sulphur and
chlorine targets.
The irradiation time was 15 min, the
beam intensity 0.02 mu-A.
The samples for irradiation were the powder of red
phosphorus and the pressed pellets of S and CsCl.
For the determination of relative curve, the
irradiation was carried out in the special target
device rotating 10 samples, and before each of them
the monitoring and absorbing foils were placed. The
absolute value of the thick target yield was
determined at the maximum energy of charged particles,
the irradiation in this case was curried out in a
target device with faraday cup. The irradiation time
was 15 min, the beam intensity 0.02 muA.
a 70 x 70 mm NaI(Tl) scintillation detector,
coupled with a 256-channel analyzer. The detector was
calibrated by standard sources of gamma-rays.
The maximum energy of the charged particle beam
was measured. The energy after the aluminum
absorbing foils was calculated using Stopping
power tables (see REL-REF).
. Stopping power
tables.
No information about source of uncertainty
. by B.V. Zatolokin.
.B.V.Zatolokin have corrected the misprintin

in Subents 002-011. BIB update. EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG.
DATA-ERR moved to Subent 001. RAD-DET deleted.
